findings.evidence: PR lines 43-156 only use the normalizer to get conjugation closure; Mathlib has the general subgroup-normal API: `normal_subgroupOf_iff_le_normalizer` at `Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Subgroup/Basic.lean:431`, `normal_in_normalizer` at line 441, and `le_normalizer_of_normal_subgroupOf` at line 450.
findings.fix: Prove a general `K ⧸ H.subgroupOf K` action/hom first, with `(hHK : H ≤ K)` and `[(H.subgroupOf K).Normal]`, then define the normalizer quotient version as the specialization `K = Subgroup.normalizer (H : Set G)`.
findings.issue: `normalizerQuotientOrbitHom` hard-codes `Subgroup.normalizer (H : Set G) ⧸ H.subgroupOf ...`, though the same orbit-quotient descent works for any subgroup `K` containing `H` with `(H.subgroupOf K).Normal`.

summary: The construction is useful, but the public API is under-generalized: it proves only the maximal normalizer case instead of the natural subgroup quotient action and then specializing to `N(H)/H`.
